title: Backport request: "Stop putting user.overlay.* into container layer" to 1.51.x
body:
The fix made in https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/eadc620 via https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1847 seems only applied to `1.53.0` onwards. This means currently buildah `1.23.x` and `1.33.x` (current on ubuntu 22.04 and 24.04 respectively) do not have fixes for these issues and thus the images produced can be broken on Docker 24, and rootless docker-in-docker in particular.

Buildah `1.23.x` seems to use storage `1.36.x` only is possibly a bit old to be touching, but would you folks consider a PR for a cherry-pick backport to `1.51.x`?

Or is the right way forward here for buildah to update to `1.53.0` onwards even on an older stable release?

comments.body:
Sorry that you have not gotten a reply so far, generally speaking we do not do a lot of backports, upstream only supports the latest version.
If you need something fixed in the old distro versions you should file a bug with them and ask them to backport it.

No problem @Luap99 - I inferred as much.

There is probably a bit too much to unpick here for an end user. Basically the `curlimages/curl` docker image was mysteriously broken on newer Docker versions at the time, because it was just being built with the buildah version available on the default ubuntu runners available in Github actions. https://github.com/curl/curl-container/issues/55 / https://github.com/curl/curl-container/issues/75

I had no choice but to switch to using a container with curl that was not being built using buildah - which is probably not ideal for buildah which is why I was trying to raise issues to help get it addresssed. I suppose curl could equally change its build process to somehow get the latest `buildah` into their build, but that also seemed a bit challenging.
what should we do specifically in curl-container - thx in advance ....

@xquery assuming this is still an issue (I haven't tried curl-container recently as gave up on it as needed something that ran within docker buildx with emulation for cross-arch builds, not just podman) but the image would need to be built using buildah 1.35+ , probably by installing latest buildah into GHA as suggested with https://github.com/curl/curl-container/issues/75

Or wait for Ubuntu 26.04 runner images whenever GitHub get around to it after release.

I do not know an easy way to do custom installation, which is why I didn't raise a PR for curl-containers back then. If buildah is statically compiled and doesn't have too many dependencies, perhaps a newer buildah version can relatively easily be installed into GHA Ubuntu 24 runners from an Ubuntu 25 or Debian repo though?

Either that or convince Ubuntu to backport the containers/storage fix to their custom buildah 1.33.x build. (which I also assessed as either unlikely or too much work)
I think we wait for gh to pick up latest ubuntu release ... which will happen before next curl release. thx for all the context.
Yeah I'd probably do that too given the time passed now, and seemingly not too many other curl users reporting the issue.
